name: Yoshitsune
description: Minamoto no Yoshitsune (1159-1189) was a military commander of the Minamoto clan of Japan during the late Heian and early Kamakura periods. He was a pivotal figure in the Genpei War, leading the Minamoto forces to several key victories against the Taira clan. Yoshitsune was the ninth son of Minamoto no Yoshitomo, and after his father's death, he was initially sheltered by Kurama Temple before later being taken in by Fujiwara no Hidehira. He distinguished himself early on with his strategic genius and prowess in battle, particularly at the Battle of Ichi-no-Tani, where he led a daring descent down a steep cliff to outflank the Taira forces. His victories continued at Yashima and Dan-no-ura, effectively ending the Genpei War and securing victory for the Minamoto clan. Despite his contributions, Yoshitsune's relationship with his elder brother, Minamoto no Yoritomo, deteriorated due to Yoritomo's suspicion and jealousy of Yoshitsune's popularity and military success. He was eventually branded a traitor and forced to flee, ultimately seeking refuge with Fujiwara no Hidehira again. After Hidehira's death, his son Yasuhira betrayed Yoshitsune, leading to Yoshitsune's tragic suicide at Koromogawa. Yoshitsune remains a legendary figure in Japanese history and folklore, celebrated for his military genius, loyalty, and tragic fate. He is often depicted as a charismatic and skilled warrior, embodying the ideals of bushido. His story has inspired countless plays, novels, and other works of art, cementing his place as one of Japan's most beloved and enduring heroes.
subtitle: The Tragic Hero of the Genpei War

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Ichi-no-Tani
keyMyths.description: Yoshitsune led a daring attack down a steep cliff to surprise and defeat the Taira forces. This unconventional strategy showcased his brilliance and bravery, securing a major victory for the Minamoto. The source of this story is the Heike Monogatari.

keyMyths.title: Yoshitsune and Benkei
keyMyths.description: Benkei, a warrior monk, became Yoshitsune's loyal retainer after being defeated in a duel. Benkei's unwavering loyalty and strength were crucial to Yoshitsune's success and their story is one of the most famous in Japanese folklore. The source is the Gikeiki.

keyMyths.title: The Tragedy at Koromogawa
keyMyths.description: Betrayed by Yasuhira, Yoshitsune was forced to commit suicide at Koromogawa. His death is a symbol of unrewarded loyalty and the tragic fate of a hero betrayed by his own clan, marking the end of a brilliant military career. The source is various historical accounts and legends.

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.description: A classic epic recounting the Genpei War.
sources.text: A classic epic recounting the Genpei War.
